title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Anthony and Virginia walk through a rose garden toward a villa, discussing a mysterious man who behaves like a hunting dog and recently provided a piece of paper allegedly dropped by a foreigner, whom Anthony identifies as Lemmon. During their walk, Anthony shares his cynical views on risk-taking and marriage, and confesses his desire to make Virginia like him, noting a sense of "magic" from their first meeting on Pound Street.

Virginia notices Constable Butler watching them from the library window. Butler informs them that Professor Woodward has arrived to decode a cipher on a letter. The group observes the red-haired professor through the window; Woodward dismisses the task as simple common sense and announces he has finished decoding the letter marked "Chimney House." After the professor departs, Butler provides Anthony and Virginia with a summary of the decoded Queen's letter.

The text reveals that an individual identified as "S"—whom Anthony identifies as Stapleton—has moved a diamond from its hiding place. The letter contains the cryptic note: "Richmond, seven straight eight left three right." While Anthony and Virginia initially consider if the diamond is in Richmond, Virginia realizes the diamond is likely hidden within the villa behind a Holbein portrait in the dining room, specifically near the portrait of the Earl of Richmond. Butler notes that thieves had previously searched the armor figures beneath the painting, suggesting the numbers in the note might refer to measurements for a secret panel or passage.
